front: Author: wrote Milton's Prosody — argues Paradise Lost not exclusively iambic pentameter; 'Neo-Miltonian syllabics'
Author: posthumously published Gerard Manley Hopkins's poems in 1918; met at Oxford
Author: wrote most popular English version of 'Jesu, Joy of Man's Desiring'; The Testament of Beauty
Author: Milton's Prosody evolved from an introduction to Paradise Lost requested by Henry Beeching
Author: Milton's Prosody Chapter II examines 'accentual verse' in Samson Agonistes
Author: demonstrates 'supernumerary syllables' with 'of man's first disobedience' from Paradise Lost Book I
Author: founded the Society for Pure English, which published dozens of reformist tracts
Author: Yvor Winters championed this poet, claiming he and Fulke Greville were better than T.S. Eliot and Spenser
Author: cousin of Digby Mackworth Dolben, whose posthumous Poems was published when this poet became Laureate
Author: The Testament of Beauty opens with 'Man's reason is in such deep insolvency to sense'
